message:
fix(doctor): fail-closed read gate when sandbox read_allow list is empty

Previously `read_allowed()` treated an empty `read_allow` list as "allow
anything not in read_deny", which silently opened reads across the entire
filesystem whenever a profile forgot to seed allow globs. Strict and Standard
profiles ship with `read_allow` empty, so this flipped the sandbox from
deny-by-default to allow-by-default on every sandbox instantiated from those
profiles without an explicit `allow_read_path` call.

Flip the predicate to fail closed: an empty allow list now denies every read
unless the caller explicitly opts paths in. The doc comment on `FsPolicy`
now says "Empty = deny all reads" to match the new contract.

Also updates the existing empty-allow-list test to cover the new behavior
(renamed to `enforcer_allows_read_not_in_deny_list_with_explicit_allow`)
and adds `enforcer_denies_read_when_allow_list_empty`, which asserts that
Strict and Standard both deny `/project/src/main.rs` with
`ViolationKind::FsReadDenied` before any explicit allows are wired in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
